title: Mobile and Ubiquitous Games
description: Design and technolfogy of mobile games, including mixed reality gaming, urban games, and locative media. Case studies of significant systems. Uses and limitations of location-based technologies. Infrastructures and their relationships to gameplay and design.
informalDescription: Design and technolfogy of mobile games, including mixed reality gaming, urban games, and locative media. Students will get hands-on practice.

title: Game Design
description: Game design takes into consideration psychology, narrative, platform features and limitations, marketing, computer science capabilities, human-computer interface principles, industry trends, aesthetic judgment, and other factors. Students focus on video game design through lectures, readings, presentations, implementation, and play testing.
informalDescription: Game design takes into consideration psychology, narrative, platform features and limitations, marketing, computer science capabilities, human-computer interface principles, industry trends, aesthetic judgment, and other factors. Students will get hands-on practice.

title: Intermediate Programming
description: Intermediate-level language features and programming concepts for larger, more complex, higher-quality software. Functional programming, name spaces, modules, class protocols, inheritance, iterators, generators, operator overloading, reflection. Analysis of time and space efficiency.

dependencies.title: Internet of Things (IoT) Software and Systems
Programming in C/C++ as a Second Language
Programming in Java as a Second Language

informalDescription: Intermediate-level language features and programming concepts for larger, more complex, higher-quality software. Students will get hands-on practice.

title: Programming in C/C++ as a Second Language
description: An introduction to the lexical, syntactic, semantic, and pragmatic characteristics of the C/C++ languages for experienced programmers. Emphasis on object-oriented programming, using standard libraries, and programming with manual garbage collection.

dependencies.title: Advanced Programming and Problem Solving with C++

informalDescription: Learn the lexical, syntactic, semantic, and pragmatic characteristics of the C/C++ languages for experienced programmers. Expect work focused on project, homework, with mixed feedback from past students.

title: Programming in Java as a Second Language
description: An introduction to the lexical, syntactic, semantic, and pragmatic characteristics of the Java language for experienced programmers. Emphasis on object-oriented programming, using standard libraries, and programming with automatic garbage collection.

dependencies.title: Project in Databases and Web Applications

informalDescription: Learn the lexical, syntactic, semantic, and pragmatic characteristics of the Java language for experienced programmers. Expect work focused on project, homework, with mixed feedback from past students.

title: Data Structure Implementation and Analysis
description: Focuses on implementation and mathematical analysis of fundamental data structures and algorithms. Covers storage allocation and memory management techniques.
informalDescription: Focuses on implementation and mathematical analysis of fundamental data structures and algorithms. Students will get hands-on practice.

title: Introductory Computer Organization
description: Multilevel view, design, and operation of computer system components. Machine-level data and instruction representation. Instruction sets and addressing modes. Memory organization. Laboratory work using low-level programming languages.
informalDescription: Multilevel view, design, and operation of computer system components. Students will get hands-on practice.

title: Principles in System Design
description: Introduces basic principles of system software: operating systems, compilers, and networking. Exposure to the following topics through theoretical and practical programming experiences: linking and loading, process and memory management, concurrency and synchronization, network communication, programming for performance, etc.
informalDescription: Explore basic principles of system software: operating systems, compilers, and networking. Students will get hands-on practice.

title: Boolean Logic and Discrete Structures
description: Relations and their properties; Boolean algebras, formal languages; finite automata.

dependencies.title: Applications of Probability in Computer Science

informalDescription: Relations and their properties; Boolean algebras, formal languages; finite automata. Students will get hands-on practice.

title: Discrete Mathematics for Computer Science
description: Covers essential tools from discrete mathematics used in computer science with an emphasis on the process of abstracting computational problems and analyzing them mathematically. Topics include mathematical induction, combinatorics, and recurrence relations.

dependencies.title: Applications of Probability in Computer Science

informalDescription: Covers and practices essential tools from discrete mathematics used in computer science with an emphasis on the process of abstracting computational problems and analyzing them mathematically. Expect work focused on homework, assignment, with mixed feedback from past students.

title: Computational Linear Algebra
description: Matrices and linear transformations, systems of linear equations, determinants, linear vector spaces, eigenvalues and eigenvectors, orthogonal matrices, diagonalization, and least squares. Topics taught primarily from an algorithmic perspective, including computational solutions, applications, and numerical error analysis.

dependencies.title: Applications of Probability in Computer Science
Artificial Intelligence in Biology and Medicine

informalDescription: Matrices and linear transformations, systems of linear equations, determinants, linear vector spaces, eigenvalues and eigenvectors, orthogonal matrices, diagonalization, and least squares. Students will get hands-on practice.

title: Advanced Programming and Problem Solving with C++
description: Advanced programming language concepts for more complex, higher performance software design. Builds depth of programming skills in C++ as a foundation for upper-division courses and projects. Focuses on strengthening programming, debugging, and problem solving skills.
informalDescription: Advanced programming language concepts for more complex, higher performance software design. Students will get hands-on practice.

title: Digital Image Processing
description: Introduction to the fundamental concepts of digital signal and image processing as applicable in areas such as multimedia, graphics, AI, data mining, databases, vision, or video games. Topics include image representation, space- and frequency-domain transformations, filters, segmentation, and compression.
informalDescription: Learn the fundamental concepts of digital signal and image processing as applicable in areas such as multimedia, graphics, AI, data mining, databases, vision, or video games. Expect work focused on assignment, exam, but students often report it as challenging.

title: Computer Graphics
description: Introduction to the fundamental principles of 3D computer graphics including polygonal modeling, geometric transformations, visibility algorithms, illumination models, texturing, and rasterization. Use of an independently-learned 3D graphics API to implement these techniques.
informalDescription: Learn the fundamental principles of 3D computer graphics including polygonal modeling, geometric transformations, visibility algorithms, illumination models, texturing, and rasterization. Expect work focused on project, homework, with mixed feedback from past students.

title: Computer Game Development
description: Introduction to the principles of interactive 2D and 3D computer game development. Concepts in computer graphics, algorithms, software engineering, ARTand graphics, music and sound, story analysis, and artificial intelligence are presented and are the basis for student work.
informalDescription: Learn the principles of interactive 2D and 3D computer game development. Expect work focused on project, assignment, and students generally give positive feedback.

title: Projects in Advanced 3D Computer Graphics
description: Projects in advanced 3D graphics such as illumination, geometric modeling, visualization, and animation. Topics include physically based and global illumination, solid modeling, curved surfaces, multiresolution modeling, image-based rendering, basic concepts of animation, and scientific visualization.
informalDescription: Work on projects in advanced 3D graphics such as illumination, geometric modeling, visualization, and animation. Students will get hands-on practice.

title: Computer Simulation
description: Discrete event-driven simulation; continuous system simulation; basic probability as pertaining to input distributions and output analysis; stochastic and deterministic simulation; static and dynamic system simulation.
informalDescription: Discrete event-driven simulation; continuous system simulation; basic probability as pertaining to input distributions and output analysis; stochastic and deterministic simulation; static and dynamic system simulation. Students will get hands-on practice.

title: Computational Photography and Vision
description: Introduces the problems of computer vision through the application of computational photography. Specific topics include photo-editing (image warping, compositing, hole filling), panoramic image stitching, and face detection for digital photographs.
informalDescription: Explore the problems of computer vision through the application of computational photography. Students will get hands-on practice.

title: Project in Computer Vision
description: Students undertake construction of a computer vision system. Topics include automatically building 3D models from photographs, searching photo collections, robot navigation, and human motion tracking.
informalDescription: Students undertake construction of a computer vision system. Expect lots of hands-on work around was, with mixed feedback from past students.

title: Introduction to Virtual Reality
description: Introduces virtual reality systems and software. Topics include computer graphics pipeline, human visual system and perception, head mounted displays, image formation, sensors and tracking, and 3D sound. Students use a popular cross-platform game engine to create VR environments.
informalDescription: Explore virtual reality systems and software. Students will get hands-on practice.

title: Information Retrieval
description: An introduction to information retrieval including indexing, retrieval, classifying, and clustering text and multimedia documents.
informalDescription: Learn information retrieval including indexing, retrieval, classifying, and clustering text and multimedia documents. Expect work focused on project, homework, with mixed feedback from past students.

title: Introduction to Data Management
description: Introduction to the design of databases and the use of database management systems (DBMS) for applications. Topics include entity-relationship modeling for design, relational data model, relational algebra, relational design theory, and Structured Query Language (SQL) programming.
informalDescription: Learn the design of databases and the use of database management systems (DBMS) for applications. Expect work focused on project, homework, with mixed feedback from past students.

title: Project in Databases and Web Applications
description: Introduces students to advanced database technologies and web applications. Topics include database connectivity (ODBC/JDBC), extending databases using stored procedures, database administration, web servers, web programming languages (Java servlets, XML, Ajax, and mobile platforms).
informalDescription: Explore students to advanced database technologies and web applications. Expect work focused on project, homework, with mixed feedback from past students.

title: Principles of Data Management
description: Covers fundamental principles underlying data management systems. Content includes key techniques including storage management, buffer management, record-oriented file system, access methods, query optimization, and query processing.
informalDescription: Covers and practices fundamental principles underlying data management systems. Expect lots of hands-on work around one, and students generally give positive feedback.

title: Beyond SQL Data Management
description: Survey of modern data management and analysis technologies beyond relational (SQL) database management. Topics include semistructured data, NoSQL databases, big data analysis, and text search/query engines. Additional topics vary and may include data frames, streaming data, and/or time-series data.
informalDescription: Survey of modern data management and analysis technologies beyond relational (SQL) database management. Students will get hands-on practice.

title: Next Generation Search Systems
description: Discusses concepts and techniques related to all aspects of search systems. After considering basic search technology and the state-of-ARTsystems, rapidly developing techniques for multimedia search, local search, event-search, and video-on-demand are explored.
informalDescription: Discusses concepts and techniques related to all aspects of search systems. Students will get hands-on practice.

title: Introduction to Security and Privacy
description: Introductory course on security mindset and basic principles/practices of software and systems security. Covers the foundations of building, using, and managing secure systems. Topics include standard security principles, threats, and defenses for real-world systems, including web, mobile, and IoT security.
informalDescription: Introductory course on security mindset and basic principles/practices of software and systems security. Expect work focused on project, exam, and students generally give positive feedback.

title: Parallel and Distributed Computing
description: Parallel and distributed computer systems. Parallel programming models. Common parallel and distributed programming issues. Specific topics include parallel programming, performance models, coordination and synchronization, consistency and replication, transactions, fault tolerance.
informalDescription: Parallel and distributed computer systems. Students will get hands-on practice.

title: Computer Networks
description: Computer network architectures, protocols, and applications. Internet congestion control, addressing, and routing. Local area networks. Multimedia networking.
informalDescription: Computer network architectures, protocols, and applications. Students will get hands-on practice.

title: Advanced Computer Networks
description: Fundamental principles in computer networks are applied to obtain practical experience and skills necessary for designing and implementing computer networks, protocols, and network applications. Various network design techniques, simulation techniques, and UNIX network programming are covered.
informalDescription: Fundamental principles in computer networks are applied to obtain practical experience and skills necessary for designing and implementing computer networks, protocols, and network applications. Students will get hands-on practice.

title: Computer and Network Security
description: Overview of modern computer and networks security, attacks, and countermeasures. Authentication, identification, data secrecy, data integrity, authorization, access control, computer viruses, network security. Also covers secure e-commerce and applications of public key methods, digital certificates, and credentials.
informalDescription: Overview of modern computer and networks security, attacks, and countermeasures. Students will get hands-on practice.

title: Internet Applications Engineering
description: Concepts in Internet applications engineering with emphasis on the Web. Peer-to-Peer and Interoperability. Topics include HTTP and REST, Remote Procedure/Method Calls, Web Services, data representations, content distribution networks, identity management, relevant W3C/IETF standards, and relevant new large-scale computing styles.
informalDescription: Concepts in Internet applications engineering with emphasis on the Web. Students will get hands-on practice.

title: Concepts in Programming Languages
description: In-depth study of several contemporary programming languages stressing variety in data structures, operations, notation, and control. Examination of different programming paradigms, such as logic programming, functional programming and object-oriented programming; implementation strategies, programming environments, and programming style.
informalDescription: In-depth study of several contemporary programming languages stressing variety in data structures, operations, notation, and control. Students will get hands-on practice.

title: Compilers and Interpreters
description: Introduction to the theory of programming language processors covering lexical analysis, syntax analysis, semantic analysis, intermediate representations, code generation, optimization, interpretation, and run-time support.
informalDescription: Learn the theory of programming language processors covering lexical analysis, syntax analysis, semantic analysis, intermediate representations, code generation, optimization, interpretation, and run-time support. Expect work focused on project, homework, with mixed feedback from past students.

title: Project in Compilers and Interpreters
description: Project course which provides working laboratory experience in construction and behavior of compilers and interpreters. Students build actual language processors and perform experiments which reveal their behaviors.
informalDescription: Project course which provides working laboratory experience in construction and behavior of compilers and interpreters. Students will get hands-on practice.

title: Principles of Operating Systems
description: Principles and concepts of process and resource management, especially as seen in operating systems. Processes, memory management, protection, scheduling, file systems, and I/O systems are covered. Concepts illustrated in the context of several well-known systems.
informalDescription: Principles and concepts of process and resource management, especially as seen in operating systems. Students will get hands-on practice.

title: Project in Operating System Organization
description: Detailed specification and design of critical components of an actual operating system including a memory manager, a process server, and a file/IO subsystem. Hardware/software tradeoffs. Emphasis on logical organization of system and communication.
informalDescription: Detailed specification and design of critical components of an actual operating system including a memory manager, a process server, and a file/IO subsystem. Students will get hands-on practice.

title: High-performance Computers and Program Optimization
description: Analyzes the relationship between computer architecture and program optimization. High-performance and parallelizing compilers for RISC, Superscalar, and VLIW architectures are discussed.
informalDescription: Analyzes the relationship between computer architecture and program optimization. Students will get hands-on practice.

title: Embedded Software
description: Principles of embedded computing systems: embedded systems architecture, hardware/software components, system software and interfacing, real-time operating systems, hardware/software co-development, and communication issues. Examples of embedded computing in real-world application domains. Simple programming using an embedded systems development environment.
informalDescription: Principles of embedded computing systems: embedded systems architecture, hardware/software components, system software and interfacing, real-time operating systems, hardware/software co-development, and communication issues. Students will get hands-on practice.

title: Programming in Multitasking Operating Systems
description: User- and systems-level programming of modern Internet-connected, multi-user, multitasking operating systems. Shells, scripting, filters, pipelines, programmability, extensibility, concurrency, inter-process communication. Concrete examples of a modern operating system (such as, but not necessarily, Unix programmed in C) are used.
informalDescription: User- and systems-level programming of modern Internet-connected, multi-user, multitasking operating systems. Students will get hands-on practice.

title: Internet of Things (IoT) Software and Systems
description: Introduction to the Internet of Things (IoT) from a systems and software perspective. IoT ecosystem including sensors, embedded CPUs, networking protocols, software, cloud services, and security and privacy requirements. IoT use cases, system design, and programming project.
informalDescription: Learn the Internet of Things (IoT) from a systems and software perspective. Expect work focused on project, assignment, and students generally give positive feedback.

title: Digital Logic Design
description: Boolean algebra. Design/analysis of combinational and sequential systems using SSI/MSI/LSI modules. Number systems. Error detecting and correction codes. Arithmetic algorithms. Hardware/firmware implementation of algorithms.
informalDescription: Boolean algebra. Students will get hands-on practice.

title: Computer Architecture
description: Design of computer elements; ALU, control unit, and arithmetic circuits. Memory hierarchy and organization. Caches. Function unit sharing and pipelining. I/O and interrupt processing. RTL and behavioral modeling using hardware description languages. Microprocessor organization and implementation techniques.
informalDescription: Design of computer elements; ALU, control unit, and arithmetic circuits. Students will get hands-on practice.

title: Computer Design Laboratory
description: Underlying primitives of computer instruction sets. Principles of microprogramming. Microprogramming. Microprograms written for one or more systems. Typical microprogramming applications discussed and implemented or simulated.
informalDescription: Underlying primitives of computer instruction sets. Students will get hands-on practice.

title: Design and Analysis of Algorithms
description: Techniques for efficient algorithm design, including divide-and-conquer and dynamic programming, and time/space analysis. Fast algorithms for problems applicable to networks, computer games, and scientific computing, such as sorting, shortest paths, minimum spanning trees, network flow, and pattern matching.
informalDescription: Techniques for efficient algorithm design, including divide-and-conquer and dynamic programming, and time/space analysis. Students will get hands-on practice.

title: Formal Languages and Automata
description: Formal aspects of describing and recognizing languages by grammars and automata. Parsing regular and context-free languages. Ambiguity, nondeterminism. Elements of computability; Turning machines, random access machines, undecidable problems, NP-completeness.
informalDescription: Formal aspects of describing and recognizing languages by grammars and automata. Students will get hands-on practice.

title: Graph Algorithms
description: Algorithms for solving fundamental problems in graph theory. Graph representations, graph traversal, network flow, connectivity, graph layout, matching problems.
informalDescription: Algorithms for solving fundamental problems in graph theory. Students will get hands-on practice.

title: Computational Geometry
description: Algorithms and data structures for computational geometry and geometric modeling, with applications to computer vision and graphics. Topics: convex hulls, Voronoi diagrams, algorithms for triangulation, motion planning, and data structures for geometric searching and modeling of 2D and 3D
informalDescription: Algorithms and data structures for computational geometry and geometric modeling, with applications to computer vision and graphics. Students will get hands-on practice.

title: Project in Algorithms and Data Structures
description: Design, implementation, execution, and analysis of algorithms for problems such as sorting, searching, data compression, and data encryption. Time-space-structure trade-offs.
informalDescription: Design, implementation, execution, and analysis of algorithms for problems such as sorting, searching, data compression, and data encryption. Students will get hands-on practice.

title: Quantum Computation and Information
description: Basic models for quantum computation and their foundations in quantum mechanics. Quantum complexity classes and quantum algorithms, including algorithms for factoring and quantum simulation. Introduction to quantum information theory and quantum entanglement.
informalDescription: Basic models for quantum computation and their foundations in quantum mechanics. Students will get hands-on practice.

title: Introduction to Applied Cryptography
description: An introduction to the essential aspects of applied cryptography, as it is used in practice. Topics include classical cryptography, block ciphers, stream ciphers, public-key cryptography, digital signatures, one-way hash functions, basic cryptographic protocols, and digital certificates and credentials.
informalDescription: Learn the essential aspects of applied cryptography, as it is used in practice. Expect work focused on homework, exam, with mixed feedback from past students.

title: Network Optimization
description: Network modeling techniques and related algorithms for solving large-scale integer programming problems. Exact methods and heuristic techniques. Applications include computer and communications networks and transportation and logistics networks.
informalDescription: Network modeling techniques and related algorithms for solving large-scale integer programming problems. Students will get hands-on practice.

title: Introduction to Optimization
description: A broad introduction to optimization. Unconstrained and constrained optimization. Equality and inequality constraints. Linear and integer programming. Stochastic dynamic programming.
informalDescription: broad Learn optimization. Expect work focused on project, homework, but students often report it as challenging.

title: Introduction to Artificial Intelligence
description: Different means of representing knowledge and uses of representations in heuristic problem solving. Representations considered include predicate logic, semantic nets, procedural representations, natural language grammars, and search trees.
informalDescription: Different means of representing knowledge and uses of representations in heuristic problem solving. Students will get hands-on practice.

title: Neural Networks and Deep Learning
description: Neural network and deep learning from multiple perspectives. Theory of parallel distributed processing systems, algorithmic approaches for learning from data in various manners, applications to difficult problems in AI from computer vision, to natural language understanding, to bioinformatics and chemoinformatics.
informalDescription: Neural network and deep learning from multiple perspectives. Expect work focused on project, assignment, but students often report it as challenging.

title: Artificial Intelligence Frontiers: Technical, Ethical, and Societal
description: Explores the frontiers of artificial intelligence and related technologies with a focus on the underlying ethical, legal, and societal challenges and opportunities they create. Encourages critical thinking about these issues.
informalDescription: Explores the frontiers of artificial intelligence and related technologies with a focus on the underlying ethical, legal, and societal challenges and opportunities they create. Students will get hands-on practice.

title: Bioinformatics
description: Introduces fundamental problems in biology that lend themselves to computational approaches. The lectures present the necessary biological background to understand the importance of the problem and the data available for algorithmic analysis.
informalDescription: Explore fundamental problems in biology that lend themselves to computational approaches. Students will get hands-on practice.

title: Project in Artificial Intelligence
description: Construction of a working artificial intelligence system. Evaluation of capabilities of the system including impact of knowledge representation.
informalDescription: Construction of a working artificial intelligence system. Students will get hands-on practice.

title: Applications of Probability in Computer Science
description: Application of probability to real-world problems in computer science. Typical topics include analysis of algorithms and graphs, probabilistic language models, network traffic modeling, data compression, and reliability modeling.
informalDescription: Application of probability to real-world problems in computer science. Students will get hands-on practice.

title: Machine Learning and Data-Mining
description: Introduction to principles of machine learning and data-mining applied to real-world datasets. Typical applications include spam filtering, object recognition, and credit scoring.
informalDescription: Learn principles of machine learning and data-mining applied to real-world datasets. Expect work focused on project, homework, with mixed feedback from past students.

title: Algorithms for Probabilistic and Deterministic Graphical Models
description: Graphical model techniques dealing with probabilistic and deterministic knowledge representations. Focuses on graphical models such as constraint networks, Bayesian networks, and Markov networks that have become a central paradigm for knowledge representation and reasoning in AI and general computer science.
informalDescription: Graphical model techniques dealing with probabilistic and deterministic knowledge representations. Students will get hands-on practice.

title: Project in Computer Science
description: Students to solve a substantial real-world problem with knowledge gained from many areas in computer science. Project has a focus on computer science but can overlap with neighbor disciplines.
informalDescription: Students to solve a substantial real-world problem with knowledge gained from many areas in computer science. Student reviews are limited; check instructors and peers for current expectations.

title: Project in Computer Science
description: Students to solve a substantial real-world problem with knowledge gained from many areas in computer science. Project has a focus on computer science but can overlap with neighbor disciplines.
informalDescription: Students to solve a substantial real-world problem with knowledge gained from many areas in computer science. Student reviews are limited; check instructors and peers for current expectations.

title: Introduction to Computational Biology
description: The use of theories and methods based on computer science, mathematics, and physics in molecular biology and biochemistry. Basics in biomolecular modeling. Analysis of sequence and structural data of biomolecules. Analysis of biomolecular functions.
informalDescription: The use of theories and methods based on computer science, mathematics, and physics in molecular biology and biochemistry. Students will get hands-on practice.

title: Artificial Intelligence in Biology and Medicine
description: Introduction to computational methods in molecular biology, aimed at those interested in learning about this interdisciplinary area. Covers computational approaches to understanding and predicting the structure, function, interactions, and evolution of DNA, RNA, proteins, and related molecules and processes.
informalDescription: Learn computational methods in molecular biology, aimed at those interested in learning about this interdisciplinary area. Expect work focused on project, homework, with mixed feedback from past students.

title: Probabilistic Modeling of Biological Data
description: A unified Bayesian probabilistic framework for modeling and mining biological data. Applications range from sequence (DNA, RNA, proteins) to gene expression data. Graphical models, Markov models, stochastic grammars, structure prediction, gene finding, evolution, DNA arrays, single- and multiple-gene analysis.
informalDescription: unified Bayesian probabilistic framework for modeling and mining biological data. Students will get hands-on practice.

title: Computational Systems Biology
description: Computational inference and modeling of gene regulation networks, signal transduction pathways, and the effects of regulatory networks in cellular processes, development, and disease. Introduction of required mathematical, computational, and data handling tools.
informalDescription: Computational inference and modeling of gene regulation networks, signal transduction pathways, and the effects of regulatory networks in cellular processes, development, and disease. Students will get hands-on practice.

title: Project in Bioinformatics
description: Teaches problem definition and analysis, data representation, algorithm design, component integration, solution validation, and testability with teams specifying, designing, building, and testing a solution to a bioinformatics problem. Lectures include engineering values, discussions, and ethical ramifications of biomedical computing issues.
informalDescription: Teaches problem definition and analysis, data representation, algorithm design, component integration, solution validation, and testability with teams specifying, designing, building, and testing a solution to a bioinformatics problem. Expect work focused on project, midterm, but students often report it as challenging.

title: Special Topics in Information and Computer Science
description: Studies in selected areas of Information and Computer Science. Topics addressed vary each quarter.
informalDescription: Studies in selected areas of Information and Computer Science. Students will get hands-on practice.

title: Individual Study
description: Individual research or investigation with Computer Science faculty.
informalDescription: Individual research or investigation with Computer Science faculty. Students will get hands-on practice.
